redFlags.evidence: Removed by Hiram Bingham's Yale expeditions in 1911–1915 under Peruvian decrees Peru maintained were loans requiring return; Yale disputed that reading for decades.
Peru — a UNESCO 1970 source country that pursued the claim through US federal court.

repatriation.note: Peru sued Yale in 2008; a 2010 agreement and a memorandum with the Universidad Nacional de San Antonio Abad del Cusco led to the material returning in three shipments across 2011–2012, where it is now held and displayed in Cusco.

journey.event: Made and deposited at the Inca site
Excavated by the Yale Peruvian Expeditions
Shipped to Yale under permits Peru treated as loans
Peru sues Yale in US federal court

weakRegisters.name: ICOM Red Lists
weakRegisters.why: Lists object categories at risk, never individual objects — a due-diligence trigger, not an identification.

weakRegisters.name: Wikidata
weakRegisters.why: Coverage follows scholarly and press attention rather than rule, so its silence carries little information.

blindRegisters.name: INTERPOL Stolen Works of Art
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft, archaeological losses; requires a theft reported to and recorded by a member-country police force.

blindRegisters.name: INTERPOL ID-Art
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft, archaeological losses; requires the same police report as the Stolen Works of Art database.

blindRegisters.name: FBI National Stolen Art File
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft losses; requires a law-enforcement submission and a value threshold.

blindRegisters.name: Carabinieri TPC
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft, archaeological losses; requires an Italian nexus — the archive is national.

blindRegisters.name: Lost Art Database
blindRegisters.why: Holds nazi-era losses; requires a loss connected to Nazi persecution, 1933–45.

blindRegisters.name: Getty Provenance Index
blindRegisters.why: Holds market, market-theft, nazi-era losses; requires passage through the European or American art market.

blindRegisters.name: Art Loss Register
blindRegisters.why: Holds market-theft, nazi-era losses; requires a registered loss, usually by an owner or insurer.

coverage.note: No register in this set can systematically name an object of this kind and origin. A quiet result here carries essentially no information about the object — it reflects where the registers look, not what happened. The strongest evidence for material like this is usually physical or archaeological, and this tool cannot see it.
coverage.comparability: This score is comparable only to other structurally uncovered objects. It must not be read against a well-covered Western-market object: the same number means 'nothing was reachable' here and 'a documented gap was found' there.
